title: DetectEntryPointReport
description:
Output ``data`` block for ``detect-entry-point`` (WS5 #4).

Wraps inside the standard SuccessEnvelope's ``data`` field (see
envelope.json). ``kind`` is ``greenfield`` when the scan found NO
entry-point candidate of any kind AND no ``@register_run`` on disk â€”
otherwise ``detected``. ``candidates`` carries one entry per discovered
entry point with its classified argv style; ``decoration_found`` lists
the files already carrying ``@register_run`` decoration. This is exactly
what ``hpc-wrap-entry-point`` SKILL.md branches on in Step 0 (scaffold
vs. onboard) and Step 1 (pick the entry point + its CLI surface).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: ``greenfield`` iff no entry-point candidate of any kind exists and no ``@register_run`` is on disk; else ``detected``. Step 0 scaffolds via ``build-template`` on ``greenfield`` and onboards the existing entry point on ``detected``.

candidates.title: Candidates
candidates.description: One entry per discovered entry-point candidate, in by-likelihood probe order. Python files carry their classified ``argv_kind`` (argparse / click / typer / hydra / fire / __main__); console scripts and shell/binary entry points carry ``console_script`` / ``shell``.

decoration_found.title: Decoration Found
decoration_found.description: Relative paths of files containing a ``@register_run`` decoration (the ``grep -rln '@register_run' notebooks/ src/ *.py`` probe). A non-empty list means the repo is already (partially) onboarded.

materialized.title: Materialized
materialized.description: The ``_materialized.entry_point`` block from ``interview.json`` when a wrapper-fallback onboarding (``hpc-wrap-entry-point``) already chose an entry point â€” ``null``/absent otherwise. Lets one ``detect-entry-point`` call answer submit.md Step 0b: branch on ``materialized.kind`` to honor a materialized wrapper, falling back to the ``candidates`` / ``decoration_found`` mature-repo probe when it is absent.

MaterializedEntryPoint.title: MaterializedEntryPoint
MaterializedEntryPoint.description: The worker-facing subset of ``interview.json``'s ``_materialized.entry_point`` block. ``kind`` selects the Step 0b procedure: ``shell_command`` short-circuits Step 1's discover scan (honor the wrapper at ``wrapper_path``, use ``executor_cmd`` as the EXECUTOR, feed ``data_axis`` into axes.yaml if present); ``register_run`` / ``python_module`` are pointers that fall through to normal discovery. Only the fields the source block declares are present (the internal ``frozen_shas`` identity detail is intentionally omitted).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: The materialized entry-point shape. ``shell_command``: a thin ``@register_run`` wrapper was materialized (the fallback path) â€” skip Step 1's discover scan. ``register_run`` / ``python_module``: pointers to a function the worker resolves through normal Step 1 discovery (no wrapper to honor).

run_name.title: Run Name
run_name.description: The wrapper / run function name (``shell_command`` and ``register_run`` kinds). For ``shell_command`` this is the materialized ``.hpc/wrappers/<run_name>.py``; treat it as the picked run.

wrapper_path.title: Wrapper Path
wrapper_path.description: POSIX-relative path to the materialized ``@register_run`` wrapper (``shell_command`` kind only), e.g. ``.hpc/wrappers/<run_name>.py``.

executor_cmd.title: Executor Cmd
executor_cmd.description: The per-task EXECUTOR command for this entry point (``shell_command`` and ``register_run`` kinds). Use it as the Step 6 ``job_env`` EXECUTOR instead of synthesizing one from ``discover-executors``.

module.title: Module
module.description: Dotted importable module path (``python_module`` kind only), e.g. ``my_pkg.train``.

function.title: Function
function.description: The function inside ``module`` to target (``python_module`` kind only).

data_axis.title: Data Axis
data_axis.description: Pre-declared series-axis classification (``shell_command`` kind, when the experimenter declared ``data_axis_hint``). When present, skip the Step 3b classification interview and feed this block into the axes.yaml write classify-axis would have done.

Candidate.title: Candidate
Candidate.description: One entry-point candidate: its path (relative to the experiment dir, or the registered command name for a console script) plus the classified argv style, plus â€” when a known solver library was recognized in the source â€” the solver adapter that can checkpoint-instrument it.

path.title: Path
path.description: Path relative to the experiment dir (e.g. ``train.py``, ``src/main.py``, ``mypkg/__main__.py``), or â€” for a ``[project.scripts]`` console script â€” the registered command name.

argv_kind.title: Argv Kind
argv_kind.description: The candidate's CLI surface. Python files: ``argparse`` / ``click`` / ``typer`` / ``hydra`` / ``fire`` (by import + decorator), or ``__main__`` for a bare ``if __name__`` block or a package ``__main__.py`` with no recognized library. ``console_script`` for a ``[project.scripts]`` entry; ``shell`` for a non-Python ``run.sh`` / ``launch.sh`` / binary.

solver.title: Solver
solver.description: Present when a known solver library's solve loop was detected in the candidate's source (AST match: petsc4py import + a PETSc.TS()/PETSc.SNES() construction + a .solve() call). Signals that the checkpoint-instrumented wrapper (``entry_point.solver`` on the interview intent) is available for this candidate â€” the library owns the iteration loop, so checkpointing injects via its monitor/options hooks rather than the executor loop helpers.
